podcast.name: Cross-Clamp Chronicles
podcast.blurb: Stories from the history of cardiac surgery and anesthesia — the people, the breakthroughs, and the hard-won lessons that shaped how we care for hearts today.

episodes.title: Blood Transfusion
episodes.summary: The birth of blood transfusion — from early concepts to future directions in cardiothoracic surgery.
notes: Early attempts at transfusion, from animal-to-human experiments to the first human-to-human transfusions.
Karl Landsteiner's discovery of the ABO blood groups (1901) and why it made transfusion safe.
Anticoagulation and storage advances that made banked blood — and modern cardiac surgery — possible.
Where it's heading: patient blood management and blood conservation in the cardiac OR.
references: Landsteiner K. Über Agglutinationserscheinungen normalen menschlichen Blutes. Wien Klin Wochenschr. 1901.

episodes.title: Norman Shumway and the Birth of Heart Transplantation
episodes.summary: A brief look into the life and career of Dr. Norman Shumway — from small beginnings in southeastern Michigan to being dubbed the Father of Heart Transplantation, a titan of medical history.
notes: Lower & Shumway's experimental work on orthotopic heart transplantation at Stanford.
Barnard's 1967 first human transplant vs. Shumway's first U.S. adult heart transplant (1968).
The long winter of transplantation and the comeback driven by endomyocardial biopsy and improved immunosuppression.
references: Lower RR, Shumway NE. Studies on orthotopic homotransplantation of the canine heart. Surg Forum. 1960.
